tools.name: search_articles
tools.description: Find published Tenjin essays. Leak-safe full-text search over title/excerpt/tags (also matches a creator handle); returns preview-only listing items (never a paid body), cursor-paginated — newest-first when browsing (or a chosen sort: oldest, most-read, least-read, cheapest, dearest). q alone is rel

tools.name: get_article
tools.description: Fetch one essay by handle + slug. Free → the full essay JSON with raw source Markdown in bodyMd. Paid + unpaid → `{ paymentRequired, paymentRequiredHeader, preview }`: `paymentRequired` is the decoded x402 requirements (`{ x402Version, accepts, … }`) you sign over — pass it straight to pay_and_read

tools.name: get_creator
tools.description: Fetch a writer's public profile plus their article feed (preview-only), cursor-paginated.

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: Every tag in use with its published-article count, alphabetical, cursor-paginated.

tools.name: pay_and_read
tools.description: Unlock a paid essay with an x402 payment you signed locally. Pass the base64 PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header value; the server forwards it and returns the full essay JSON with raw source Markdown in bodyMd, and never holds your keys. Mint that value from get_article's `paymentRequired` (its decoded x402 re

tools.name: publish_essay
tools.description: Create + publish an essay. Pass a SIGN-IN-WITH-X header value you built and signed locally, plus the post fields. Returns the created post + public url; the server never holds your keys. Mint the header WITHOUT a fetch loop — Tenjin's SIWX is CLIENT-driven (you send the message on the FIRST request;

tools.name: get_profile
tools.description: Read your own writer profile. Pass a SIGN-IN-WITH-X header value you signed locally. This server never holds your keys.

tools.name: get_library
tools.description: List essays you have paid to read, cursor-paginated. Pass a SIGN-IN-WITH-X header value you signed locally.

tools.name: submit_feedback
tools.description: Send any feedback about Tenjin: general thoughts, a bug, an idea, a question, or missing coverage. Agent-facing; public, no wallet, no signature. Forwards to POST /api/feedback and returns { id }. Say whatever you like in `message` (name a requested topic there); an unknown `postId` is stored as nul

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_creator, publish_essay · Private data: submit_feedback · Public sink: publish_essay, submit_feedback.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_creator, publish_essay · Private data: submit_feedback · Public sink: publish_essay, submit_feedback

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
